title: Curator Desk
text:
Welcome to your central workspace for refining PubMed literature discovery. This dashboard bridges the gap between AI-driven BioAnalyzer predictions and expert human insight, putting a searchable, high-performance table of candidate articles at your fingertips.
As you review and capture ground truth for each PMID, the system works in the background to store statuses and track correctness flags in real-time. This seamless integration of human feedback allows you to:

Streamline Validation: Effortlessly navigate and filter high-priority candidates.
Refine Model Accuracy: Log curator ground truth to build a robust data foundation.
Generate High-Level Analytics: Export data ready for deep-dive error profiling, confusion matrices, and MCC performance metrics to help evaluate BioAnalyzer package accuracy.







Data Source & Setup





The table is built from data stored in the data/ folder. To analyze your own papers:

Place your CSV (or Parquet) file in data/ (e.g., data/sample.csv).
Run quarto render.

The system reads data/sample.csv by default. To use a different source, set the CURATOR_DATA_PATH environment variable to your specific path (e.g., data/analyzed_papers.csv) or use CURATOR_DATA_URL for remote files.
Expected Columns: * Required: PMID * Recommended: Title, Journal, Year, Summary * Status Values: ABSENT | PARTIALLY_PRESENT | PRESENT







Interactive Table


Tip: Sort by Priority Score (descending) to review the most promising candidates first. Use the search box and column filters to narrow your focus.






Rows in table: 30


Use the table above to find papers (the Open button in the Published column opens the paper on PubMed). Then select or enter a PMID below; the page will scroll to the field-by-field validation form where you can enter your review. Feedback uses the same schema as the Streamlit app; because this site is static, saving is done by downloading a CSV per row—submit via your usual process (e.g. email or GitHub issue).

Quick select a PMID from the dropdown, or type a PMID and click Load row (the form will scroll into view).
For each field, review BioAnalyzer predicted and set Curator TRUE label and Was BioAnalyzer correct?
Click Download feedback CSV to save one row; submit as needed.





Curator feedback form



Curator ID / initials

Submit review — Opens GitHub to create an issue with your feedback (no file download). A bot will then open a Pull Request with the feedback CSV so the maintainer can view and download it (including your initials and comments).


Submit review


Download CSV only



Notes: Submissions are stored in the repo folder curator-feedback/. Set BIOANALYZER_VERSION or GITHUB_REPO (e.g. https://github.com/owner/repo) in the environment when building.






Thank you for your time and input in helping to complete this review.
Your feedback has been submitted; an issue and pull request are being created in the background.
Would you like to continue reviewing other papers?


Yes
